refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time for each phase of the load→travel→dump cycle to meet or exceed the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: Decomposing the overall cycle time KPI into sub-functions for each phase (load, travel, dump) allows targeted design parameters and implementation modules to optimize each phase, improving overall cycle time performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time for each phase under representative duty cycles and site conditions, then aggregate to verify overall cycle time target compliance.

candidate_dps: Optimize load mechanism speed and coordination
Optimize travel path planning and vehicle speed
Optimize dump mechanism actuation and timing
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize load mechanism speed and coordination; Optimize travel path planning and vehicle speed; Optimize dump mechanism actuation and timing

potential_sources: Interdependency between travel path planning and load/dump timing
Coordination between mechanical actuation and vehicle control
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically separable by cycle phase but require interface coordination to avoid timing conflicts; coupling is manageable with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor data and telemetry for timing optimization
Risk of suboptimal coordination causing cycle time delays
High safety criticality requires fail-safe timing controls
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit interface definitions between load, travel, and dump DPs to reduce coupling risk.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components responsible for load, travel, and dump mechanisms

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends cycle phase status and timing data to NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives commands for load, travel, and dump operations from NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends safety alerts related to cycle operations to Safety Services

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle module contains all physical mechanisms for cycle phases, minimizing diffusion.
NGHE Solution manages overall coordination but does not contain physical actuation.
Teleoperation module is peripheral to cycle time optimization.
Safety Services only receives alerts, no direct containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning cycle time optimization DPs to the Vehicle module centralizes physical actuation control, reducing diffusion and improving timing coordination.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle module due to multiple cycle phase controls
Coordination with NGHE Solution for tasking and telemetry may introduce interface latency
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface protocols between Vehicle and NGHE Solution to mitigate latency and coordination risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement logically decomposes cycle time KPI into manageable sub-functions; selected DPs address each sub-FR with clear optimization focus.

diffusion_risks: Moderate diffusion risk if Vehicle module becomes overly complex
Interface latency between Vehicle and NGHE Solution could impact timing
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module scores highest containment for physical actuation DPs, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring cycle times and telemetry data from Vehicle module and correlating with NGHE Solution commands.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of physical actuation to Vehicle and coordination to NGHE Solution supports traceability.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface definitions between Vehicle and NGHE Solution to reduce latency and improve coordination.
Consider modularizing Vehicle controls internally if complexity grows.

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments meet requirements with manageable risks; verification and ownership are clear; no immediate need to zigzag or revise.

revise.why: No critical issues identified requiring revision at this level.

assumptions: Accurate telemetry and tasking interfaces are available and reliable.
Vehicle module can handle integrated control of load, travel, and dump mechanisms.
open_risks: Interface latency between Vehicle and NGHE Solution may affect cycle time optimization.
Complexity in Vehicle module control logic could increase maintenance burden.
evidence_needed: Performance data validating cycle time improvements per phase.
Interface latency measurements between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Safety validation of timing controls under high safety criticality.
